findings.message: Docstring claims to return 'the total number of items in the catalogue', but the implementation calls list_items(session) with no limit override. list_items defaults to DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE=50 and is hard-capped at MAX_PAGE_SIZE=100, so the count is silently bounded — it is not a true total.
findings.suggestion: Either fix the implementation to use a real aggregate query (e.g. session.scalar(select(func.count()).select_from(Item))) and keep the docstring, or reword the docstring to reflect the bounded behaviour (e.g. 'Return the number of items visible in the default page (up to DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE)').

findings.message: Endpoint entry reads '— total items in the catalogue' but the implementation is bounded by list_items' DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE, so callers relying on this description for a true row count will be misled.
findings.suggestion: Align the README description with whatever the docstring and implementation settle on — either 'total items' (if the implementation is corrected to a COUNT query) or note the bound explicitly.

findings.message: A new route (GET /items/count) is added but openapi.json does not appear to be updated in this diff. Per the README CI/CD section, CI runs an OpenAPI contract-diff job and fails on a stale or breaking spec.
findings.suggestion: Run 'task openapi:export', commit the regenerated openapi.json alongside this change, and verify 'task ci' passes locally before pushing.
